<div align="center">

# 🔥 Forge

### Build full-stack apps by just describing them.

**Forge** is an AI-powered app builder that turns natural-language prompts into fully working web applications — generated by Gemini, previewed live in an in-browser sandbox, and exportable with one click.

[![Next.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-16-black?logo=next.js)](https://nextjs.org/)
[![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-19-61DAFB?logo=react&logoColor=white)](https://react.dev/)
[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5-3178C6?logo=typescript&logoColor=white)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
[![Prisma](https://img.shields.io/badge/Prisma-7-2D3748?logo=prisma&logoColor=white)](https://www.prisma.io/)
[![Tailwind CSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tailwind-v4-38B2AC?logo=tailwind-css&logoColor=white)](https://tailwindcss.com/)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-A78BFA.svg)](#license)

[Live Demo](https://forge-gold-nine.vercel.app) · [Report a Bug](../../issues) · [Request a Feature](../../issues)

</div>

---

## ✨ Overview

Forge lets you describe an app in plain English and watch it come to life. Behind the scenes, Google's **Gemini** models generate the code, which renders instantly in an embedded **CodeSandbox Sandpack** preview — no local setup, no boilerplate, no waiting.

Keep refining your app conversationally, see every change reflected in real time, and when you're happy with the result, export the whole project as a ready-to-use **ZIP** file.

<div align="center">

| 💬 Describe | ⚡ Generate | 👀 Preview | 📦 Export |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Tell Forge what to build | Gemini writes the code | See it live in-browser | Download as a ZIP |

</div>

## 🚀 Features

| | Feature | Description |
|---|---------|--------------|
| 🤖 | **AI Code Generation** | Describe your app in plain English — Gemini generates the full project structure and code. |
| ⚡ | **Live Sandbox Preview** | Your generated app runs instantly in an embedded sandbox, no local setup required. |
| 🔄 | **Conversational Iteration** | Refine, extend, and fix your app through natural follow-up prompts. |
| 🔐 | **Authentication** | Secure sign-up, sign-in, and session management powered by Clerk. |
| 🗄️ | **Persistent Projects** | Your apps and history are saved via Prisma + PostgreSQL (Supabase). |
| 📦 | **One-Click Export** | Download your generated project as a portable ZIP archive. |
| 🎨 | **Polished UI** | Built with shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS v4, and fluid Motion animations. |
| 🛡️ | **Abuse Protection** | Rate limiting and bot detection powered by Arcjet. |

## 🛠️ Tech Stack

<div align="center">

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| **Framework** | Next.js 16 (App Router) |
| **UI Library** | React 19 |
| **Language** | TypeScript |
| **AI Engine** | Google Gemini (`@google/genai`) |
| **Live Sandbox** | CodeSandbox Sandpack |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL (Supabase) |
| **ORM** | Prisma 7 |
| **Authentication** | Clerk |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS v4 · shadcn/ui · Lucide Icons |
| **Animation** | Motion (Framer Motion) |
| **Security** | Arcjet |
| **Export** | JSZip |

</div>

## 📦 Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- **Node.js** 18+
- A **PostgreSQL** database (e.g. [Supabase](https://supabase.com))
- API keys for **[Clerk](https://clerk.com)**, **[Google Gemini](https://ai.google.dev)**, and **[Arcjet](https://arcjet.com)**

### 1. Clone the repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dndmein-rgb/Forge.git
cd Forge
```

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

> Prisma's client is generated automatically via the `postinstall` script.

### 3. Configure environment variables

Create a `.env` file in the project root:

```env
# Database
DATABASE_URL=

# Clerk Authentication
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=
CLERK_SECRET_KEY=

# Google Gemini
GEMINI_API_KEY=

# Arcjet
ARCJET_KEY=
```

> ⚠️ Variable names above reflect the services used by this project — double check exact key names against your own `.env` setup if one exists in the repo, since it wasn't included in version control.

### 4. Run the development server

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) and start building. 🎉

## 🗂️ Project Structure
Forge/

├── app/          # Next.js App Router pages & API routes

├── components/   # Reusable UI components

├── lib/          # Utilities, AI logic, and shared helpers

├── prisma/       # Database schema & migrations

├── types/        # Shared TypeScript types

├── actions/      # Server actions

└── public/       # Static assets
